rulings.Case Title: JOHN MCHUGH et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel St. Francis Hotel Corporation To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, September 05, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel St. Francis Hotel Corp. to produce custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents is continued to September 10, 2013 per agreement of the parties. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRELL FORTNER et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: A Hearing On An Order To Show Cause On Plaintiffs' Failure To Comply With Protective Order (Per Ex Parte Order Dated 8/22/13)
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Order to show cause regarding plaintiffs¿ failure to comply with protective order: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY R.F. COLLINS VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petro-Chemical Insulation, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents And Requests For Admissions; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 05, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Petro-Chemical Insulation Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, requests for production of documents and requests for admissions and request for monetary sanctions is continued to September 19, 2013 per stipulation and order of August 28, 2013. = (503/TLJ)
